fields.narrative_intro: *A smug person and a deranged person discuss computer-use agents versus structured APIs on a runaway train.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
SMUG PERSON: "Our vision agent can use any website. No APIs needed."
[Visual: Smug person gestures at a laptop showing a browser with seventeen open tabs.]
fields.panel2:
[build]
DERANGED PERSON: "How's the admin panel task going?"
SMUG PERSON: "Fifty-three steps. Seventeen minutes. Very thorough."
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
DERANGED PERSON: "The direct API did it in eight calls. Twenty seconds."
SMUG PERSON: "...It missed data below the fold."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: The deranged person holds up a printout: '551,000 INPUT TOKENS.' The smug person stares at it. The train screams past a sign reading '45X CHEAPER.' Neither blinks.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
DERANGED PERSON: "So you paid to watch it fail to scroll down."
SMUG PERSON: "We gave it a fourteen-step walkthrough. It succeeded eventually."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: A framed poster on the train wall reads: 'An agent that must see in order to act will always pay for the seeing.' It is on fire. The smug person is nodding at it approvingly.]

narrative_summary: A smug computer-use evangelist is confronted with the specific, damning numbers from the benchmarking study — 53 steps, 551k tokens, a scroll-down failure — while a deranged skeptic delivers the verdict perfectly flat on a runaway train.
rationale: Candidate 1 earns its edge through the silent panel 4 — holding up the '551,000 INPUT TOKENS' printout while neither character blinks is a genuine visual beat, not just a restatement. The closing image of the smug person approvingly nodding at a burning poster is a sharper button than the other two, which both end on the same 'train on fire' gag without the ironic character payoff.
story_title: Computer Use is 45x more expensive than structured APIs
hat_pair: viking helmet with tall curving horns
